bead.title: Avoid unreachable Vidar route after provider_connectivity failure
bead.description:
PROBLEM
A Niflheim ddx work attempt for bead niflheim-27416d39 reached execution and then failed after DDx/Fizeau routed the continuation to provider vidar model Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit. The route failed with provider_connectivity: openai POST http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions dial tcp 100.108.162.80:1235 i/o timeout.

IMPACT
The project queue remains worker-ready, but attempts can fail after useful work if the default/cheap route selects an unreachable Vidar endpoint. This is infrastructure, not a Niflheim acceptance failure.

EVIDENCE
Niflheim attempt id: 20260518T190003-d738a271. Worker output ended with: provider_connectivity, provider=vidar, model=Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit, endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions, i/o timeout.

PROPOSED FIX
Make provider health or route selection avoid unreachable Vidar endpoints, or mark Vidar unhealthy before dispatch so DDx falls back to a reachable provider. Add regression coverage for provider-connectivity failures so a failed route is recorded and retried/falls back without consuming the useful attempt.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Niflheim beads. Do not mask genuine task failures as provider failures.
bead.acceptance:
1. A diagnostic or health check marks an unreachable Vidar endpoint unavailable before DDx/Fizeau selects it for work.
2. A provider_connectivity failure records actionable route facts including provider, model, endpoint, and timeout class.
3. A retry/fallback path avoids repeatedly selecting the same unreachable Vidar route for the same bead attempt.
4. Focused tests for provider connectivity failure handling pass.
5. Repository pre-commit or equivalent local gate passes.
